title: Q*Bird Launches Falqon Key Manager to Integrate Hybrid QKD and Post-Quantum Cryptography - Quantum Computing Report
gist: Delft-based Q*Bird launched the Falqon Key Manager, a QKMS appliance unifying QKD, post-quantum cryptography, and classical PKI in one crypto-agile framework, shifting the market from point-to-point links to multi-vendor networks and pivoting Q*Bird from proof-of-concept to commercial deployment.

insights.claim: Q*Bird's Falqon Key Manager natively unifies Quantum Key Distribution, Post-Quantum Cryptography, and classical PKI in a single crypto-agile framework, mitigating math-based PQC's algorithmic vulnerabilities while extending interception-immune quantum security beyond dedicated dark fiber into third-party cloud and OT edge nodes.
insights.speaker: Mohamed Abdel-Kareem
insights.so_what: Combining QKD's physical security with PQC hedges against either approach failing; a defensible architecture for defense/telecom buyers.
insights.locator: The underlying system architecture natively unifies

insights.claim: The launch shifts the industry focus from isolated point-to-point QKD links toward highly interconnected, multi-vendor networks, with the appliance sitting between raw key-generation hardware and commercial data-consuming applications.
insights.speaker: Mohamed Abdel-Kareem
insights.so_what: Signals maturation of the QKD market toward scalable orchestration; multi-vendor interoperability becomes the competitive frontier.
insights.quote: the hardware deployment shifts the industry focus from isolated point-to-point links toward highly interconnected, multi-vendor networks

insights.claim: Falqon relies on Q*Bird's Measurement-Device-Independent QKD (MDI-QKD) routing layer to drive multipoint-to-multipoint key generation without requiring vulnerable physical trusted-node chains across intermediate data centers.
insights.speaker: Mohamed Abdel-Kareem
insights.so_what: Eliminating trusted-node vulnerability is a genuine security edge over conventional QKD; a differentiator to weigh against competitors.
insights.locator: Co-engineered by Chief Technology Officer Dr. Joshua Slater

insights.claim: The appliance supports decentralized deployment (embedded in existing Q*Bird nodes, standalone on-premises hardware, or virtualized secure containers in private cloud), enabling automated key lifecycle management and preventing vendor lock-in through standards-based interfaces.
insights.speaker: Mohamed Abdel-Kareem
insights.so_what: Anti-lock-in, standards-based positioning appeals to government/defense procurement; supports adoption across compliance regimes.

insights.claim: Q*Bird, a Delft University of Technology spin-off led by CEO Dr. Ingrid Romijn, is pivoting from exploratory proof-of-concept testing to high-throughput commercial deployment, positioning as a European infrastructure provider for cross-border data sovereignty and the upcoming European quantum internet.
insights.speaker: Mohamed Abdel-Kareem
insights.so_what: European quantum-sovereignty positioning aligns with EU strategic autonomy funding; watch for public-sector contracts.
insights.quote: the Delft University of Technology spin-off is pivoting from exploratory proof-of-concept testing to high-throughput commercial deployment
insights.locator: Led by Chief Executive Officer and Co-Founder Dr. Ingrid Romijn
